prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel vehicle called a klon has a property: it can be activated by one of its colored panels. Your job is to figure out which vehicle in the perception scenario is the klon-analog, and therefore which colored panel on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three vehicles in a lot: a klon, a vro, and a yib. The klon is-parked-beneath the vro. The vro is-parked-to-the-left-of the yib. The klon has a purple colored panel on top, a blue colored panel on side, and a yellow colored panel on bottom. The vro has a blue colored panel on top and a purple colored panel on bottom. The yib has a yellow colored panel on top and a purple colored panel on side. Switching the purple panel on the klon activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three vehicles in a lot: a jax, a glim, and a oxim. The glim is-parked-beneath the jax. The jax is-parked-to-the-left-of the oxim. The jax has a blue colored panel on top and a purple colored panel on side. The glim has a purple colored panel on top, a blue colored panel on side, and a yellow colored panel on bottom. The oxim has a yellow colored panel on top and a blue colored panel on bottom.

Which vehicle in the perception scenario is the klon-analog, and which colored panel activates it?
